message:
beads: sync — wa-1u90p.1.1 closed, wa-1u90p.1.2/1.3/1.6 progress

Issue tracker updates from concurrent agent activity:
- wa-1u90p.1.1 closed (resize baseline scenarios complete)
- wa-1u90p.1.2 assigned to IndigoMountain (resize timeline instrumentation)
- wa-1u90p.1.3 assigned to DustySnow (lock contention profiling)
- wa-1u90p.1.6 assigned to HazyHarbor (frankentui audit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1","title":"Baseline Forensics, SLOs, and Bottleneck Attribution","description":"Establish reproducible worst-case workloads, hard performance SLOs, and evidence-based bottleneck attribution before deeper architectural changes.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks for this track are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Track-level objectives are met with measurable evidence (correctness + performance where applicable).
- Validation artifacts and operational notes are captured for future maintainers.","design":"Baseline-first evidence track","acceptance_criteria":"All baseline artifacts complete and approved","notes":"Downstream tracks depend on this evidence","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.159442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:29:43.308530Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","performance","reflow","resize","track-baseline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.159442Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Track 1 Detailed Intent: Baseline Forensics and SLO Grounding

This track creates the empirical foundation for the entire program.

## Why this track exists
Without reproducible scenarios and stage-level attribution, optimization effort drifts into guesswork. This track forces a measurable starting point.

## Required outputs
- deterministic stress scenarios
- resize timeline instrumentation (with queue/phase visibility)
- lock/memory profiling under storm load
- SLO/quality-gate document
- ranked bottleneck dossier
- frankentui reuse matrix (Knuth-Plass, scratch buffers, virtualization ideas)

## What “done” means
Any downstream implementation task should be able to point to this track’s artifacts and justify why it is expected to move specific metrics.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:28:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1.1","title":"Reproduce canonical worst-case resize/font-change scenarios across pane/tab/scrollback scales","description":"Build deterministic scenario suites that stress window resize, font-size churn, mux server load, and extreme scrollback across many panes/tabs. Capture scenario metadata so runs are reproducible and comparable over time.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoMountain","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:43.699441Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:10:45.344142Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T04:10:45.344127Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic resize/font-change baseline scenarios and simulation metadata surface; full workspace quality gates currently blocked by unrelated pre-existing build failures (missing search module + existing -D warnings set).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","performance","reflow","resize","track-baseline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:43.699441Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":54,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.1","author":"IndigoMountain","text":"Implemented deterministic resize/font-change scenario baseline artifacts via simulation layer updates and simulate CLI surfacing. Added Scenario metadata/reproducibility key, window/tab pane fields, SetFontSize + GenerateScrollback actions, and validation for new specs in crates/frankenterm-core/src/simulation.rs. Updated ft simulate outputs/listing in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and added/validated suite fixtures + docs + integration test file already present in repo (fixtures/simulations/resize_baseline/*, docs/resize-baseline-scenarios.md, crates/frankenterm-core/tests/simulation_resize_suite.rs). Quality gate attempt blocked by pre-existing workspace issues unrelated to this bead (missing module crates/frankenterm-core/src/search.rs via pub mod search in lib.rs, plus existing -D warnings failures).","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:10:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","title":"Define hard SLOs: frame-time, interaction latency, artifact tolerance, and crash budget","description":"## Context
All later implementation and rollout decisions depend on explicit, measurable targets that reflect user perception and operational reliability.

## Scope
Define the authoritative SLO/SLA-style gate set for resize/reflow work, including frame pacing, interaction latency, artifact classes, and crash budgets.

## Deliverables
- SLO specification with p50/p95/p99 thresholds per workload class
- Hardware-tier matrix (low/mid/high) with expected behavior envelopes
- Pass/fail gate definitions for CI, soak, and go/no-go review
- Exception/degradation policy with trigger conditions and recovery criteria

## Why this matters
Without hard targets, optimization becomes subjective and release risk increases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SLO document defines explicit numeric thresholds for frame-time, interaction latency, artifact incidence, and crash-free operation.
- Thresholds are mapped to specific benchmark/e2e harnesses and artifact outputs.
- Low/mid/high hardware tier expectations are explicit and testable.
- Degradation/rollback triggers are documented and referenced by rollout beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.013858Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:53:49.592084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reflow","resize","slo","track-baseline","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.013858Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.693879Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.798467Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
